metadata.title: Project Spec
metadata.purpose: This file, together with the other docs under harness/project-spec/, is the authoritative invariant space for harnessed work in this repo.

project_is: A local Python/JSON coding-agent CLI harness.

A system that orchestrates clean-context coding agents through the OpenAI Agents SDK by treating role contracts, context packets, governance policies, output schemas, runtime reports, and acceptance evidence as explicit structured artifacts.
A harness exists to make agent-assisted software work inspectable, bounded, reproducible, and governed.
project_is_not: A replacement for repo-specific project specs.
A framework for giving an LLM broad ambient access to files, shell commands, or tools.
An agent “team” simulation where agents freely call each other.
A prompt collection where behavior depends on conversational momentum.
A hidden-memory system that treats prior chat as implementation authority.
The harness does not try to make coding agents more magical. It tries to make their participation in software work more explicit, auditable, and bounded.
core_runtime_shape: A CLI entrypoint receives a user task.
The orchestrator selects a bounded workflow.
The context compiler produces a structured context packet.
Role contracts define what each agent is allowed to do and how it should interpret the packet.
The OpenAI API is used for bounded semantic transformation calls.
Report schemas define machine-checkable output shapes.
Runtime probes and saved artifacts distinguish scaffold claims from live-wired behavior.
Worker adapters are isolated from governance roles and require explicit authorization before file mutation.
The system is a governance runtime first, not an autonomy demo.

delineate_boundaries: Chat memory versus repo-local implementation state.
instantiate_governance: Controlling what context enters each agent call.
Separating role semantics from runtime mechanics.
Forcing structured reports instead of vague advice.
Recording evidence of what actually happened.
Preventing implementation from being treated as admissible merely because an agent wrote a plausible plan.
Keeping review and proof tied to repo-local artifacts, not chat history.

harness_must_respect: The current filesystem state of the repo.
The current git state where available.
The structured role contracts under harness/agents/**.
Explicit user approval for approval-boundary crossings.

desired_user_outcomes.primary_operator: Primary operator is the project authority using agent assistance to build software wanting stronger control over context, authority, permissions, and evidence than a normal chat interface or IDE extension provides.

expectations: Compile repo-local context into a structured context packet.
load the relevant role contract.
Call the Project Manager route through the OpenAI Agents SDK.
Receive a structured admissibility report.
Validate that report against the schema.
Save the task brief, context packet, and report under harness/runs/<timestamp>/.
Print a short terminal summary.
Identify the next admissible transition.
Stop before implementation unless an apply workflow has been explicitly invoked and authorized.
User start a governed coding session from the terminal.
User asks for a PM admissibility review of a task.
User sees what context was sent to the agent.
User sees which sources were included or missing.
User sees what role contract governed the agent.
User sees which model was called.
User sees what output schema constrained the report.
User sees whether the report validated.
User sees whether the deterministic runtime probe passed.
User sees the next admissible transition.
System stops before implementation by default.
Later authorized bounded implementation for an explicit apply phase.
evidence_required: The exact structured context packet sent.
The exact schema used for validation.
Whether a claim is scaffold-only, spec-level, or runtime-evidenced.
Which surfaces were affected or not affected.
useful_vs_plausible: The CLI can be run repeatedly with predictable artifact output.
Each run leaves a repo-local evidence trail.
The PM reliably surfaces the dominant tension instead of inventing a task list.
Missing authority is named rather than smoothed over.
Implementation does not begin by accident.
Schemas fail loudly when reports drift.
Context packets are structured enough to inspect and transform.
Policy and role boundaries remain visible.
The system can govern its own next implementation step without relying on chat memory.
The system is not useful merely because it produces a sophisticated-looking answer, it is useful when that answer is bounded, validatable, and tied to the next evidence-producing transformation.
The harness should make the difference between “the agent said it” and “the runtime proved it” impossible to miss.

do_not_build: Automatic repo mutation from a planning command.
Broad shell/tool access for governance agents.
A vector database or RAG system.
Model-provider abstraction beyond what is needed for the first OpenAI SDK path.
Compatibility shims for earlier scaffold layouts unless explicitly approved.
Fake scaffolding, fixtures, mocks, dry runs, or structure alone.
A system that smooths over ambiguity, uncertainty, or missing evidence with polished wording. The system must preserve uncertainty.
A system that preserves legacy behavior, donor-code assumptions, or compatibility shims unless explicitly required.

required_runtime_surfaces: The first working implementation may be Python-only.
The first durable representation of governance should be JSON-first where the source is object-shaped.
required_evidence_boundaries: Project spec versus current user task.
Model-generated claim versus deterministic probe result.
Archived history versus active implementation state.
